channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: 11-year-old Pasadena student forced to duct tape her own mouth
item.description: PASADENA -- (KTLA) The parents of an 11-year-old girl are outraged after a school employee forced her to duct tape her own mouth for reportedly speaking too much. The child involved, Noemi Hurtado, is a sixth-grade student at Blair Middle School in Pasadena. On Mar.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Steph Curry scores a 3-book deal, starting with a "personal reflection" on his life and career
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steph Curry has a deal with a Random House Publishing Group imprint that you could call a 3-point play. The NBA superstar has agreed to release three books through One World, beginning Sept.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Disney failed to protect guests from employee taking ‘upskirt’ photos, lawsuit alleges
item.description: A new lawsuit alleges that Disney failed to protect guests from employee taking "upskirt" photos of guests at its Florida theme park in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Myanmar's earthquake death toll jumps to 1,644 as more bodies are recovered from the rubble
item.description: Myanmar’s ruling military confirmed death toll from a devastating 7.7 magnitude earthquake rose to 1,644, as more bodies were pulled from the rubble of the scores of buildings that collapsed.
item.dc:creator: David Rising, KREX 5

item.title: Wisconsin judge won't stop Musk's $1 million payments to voters. Attorney general appeals
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A Wisconsin judge on Saturday declined to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters in the state at a planned rally days before the closely contested Supreme Court election.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Authorities find skeletal remains at home of Georgia man who died in Hawaii
item.description: The family of a 74-year-old man who died during a trip to Hawaii say they found the skeletal remains of a human being in a treehouse on his Georgia property a few days after his death.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, NewsNation

item.title: Danish foreign minister scolds Trump administration for its criticism of Denmark and Greenland
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — The Danish foreign minister on Saturday scolded the Trump administration for its “tone” in criticizing Denmark and Greenland, saying his country is already investing more into Arctic security and remains open to more cooperation with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP CROWTHER, KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: UPS launching online tool to show tariff costs to customers
item.description: “Until now, international purchases often arrived with an unpleasant surprise – an additional bill for unpaid import costs.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Jersey's GOP primary for governor could pivot on Trump, a part-time resident
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — The most important Republican in New Jersey's race for governor this year might well be a part-time resident of Bedminster who burnished his reputation and his brand near the Atlantic City Boardwalk.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Vance accuses Denmark of underinvesting in Greenland as Trump presses for US takeover of the island
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — U.S. Vice President JD Vance said Friday that Denmark has "underinvested" in Greenland’s security and demanded that Denmark change its approach as President Donald Trump pushes to take over the Danish territory. The pointed remarks came as Vance visited U.S.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP CROWTHER, KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Transgender teens fight back against Trump's ban on participation in girls' sports
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — Parker Tirrell, 16, enjoys her art classes, scrolling on TikTok and working at her new job at a pet store. But most of all, the transgender teen loves playing soccer. Until last year, that wasn’t a problem.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and RODRIQUE NGOWI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CEO who owned rights to Pablo Escobar’s name arrested for fraud, money laundering
item.description: The CEO of a company that owned the rights to Colombian drug lord Pablo Escobar’s name and likeness was arrested and extradited to L.A. to face fraud and money laundering charges. Olaf Kyros Gustafsson, 31, also known as “El Silencio,” is a Swedish national who owned Escobar Inc.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KTLA 5

item.title: Columbia University's interim president steps down and returns to former post
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Columbia University’s interim president Katrina Armstrong has resigned, returning to her post running the New York school’s medical center.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Texas reaches 400 measles cases as US deals with outbreaks in 5 states
item.description: At least five states have active measles outbreaks as of Friday, and Texas' is the largest with 400 cases.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Columbia University interim president steps down amid clash with Trump admin over federal funding
item.description: Columbia University Interim President Katrina Armstrong is stepping down from her role leading the school, with board of trustees co-chair Claire Shipman taking her place.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stolen truck pursuit ends with violent, multi-vehicle pileup in Southern California
item.description: One of the more dangerous and destructive police pursuits in recent memory unfolded on live television in south Los Angeles County Friday afternoon, involving a driver in a white stolen pickup truck.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Patient dies after getting rabies from organ transplant
item.description: Michigan Department of Health and Human Services spokesperson Lynn Sutfin said there is no threat to the general public.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Man suspected of setting fire to Tesla vehicles in Las Vegas arrested, police say
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A man suspected of setting fire to Tesla vehicles in Las Vegas and painting the word “resist” on the service center's doors has been arrested, police announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Baby's corpse, busted casket found on grounds of West Virginia cemetery
item.description: A baby's corpse and a busted casket were found at a cemetery in Salt Rock, West Virginia, on Thursday evening, according to the Cabell County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Barren, KRON4

item.title: This is the top-rated seafood restaurant in California, according to Yelp
item.description: There are plenty of fish in the sea, as the old saying goes. Unfortunately, there are far fewer exceptional chefs who can cook one for dinner. Knowing this, the analysts at Yelp have sought to identify the top-rated seafood restaurants in each state.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Tufts University student arrest: Top Dems want answers from Noem, Rubio, ICE on 'deeply troubling' incident
item.description: Top Democrats are calling for the release of Tufts University graduate student Rumeysa Ozturk, writing a letter to Trump administration officials demanding answers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Video shows Bangkok high-rise collapse during magnitude 7.7 earthquake
item.description: A devastating earthquake struck Myanmar and neighboring Thailand on Friday, resulting in the deaths of at least 150 people, primarily in Myanmar. The quake caused the collapse of a bridge and a dam.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Juror in 1st Karen Read trial joins defense, says case shook faith in criminal justice system
item.description: A Massachusetts attorney who served as an alternate juror in the mistrial of Karen Read last year has joined her defense in her second murder trial.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NFL legend Randy White fights fentanyl after personal tragedy
item.description: Jesse Dempsey, a professional snowboarder, unknowingly took a pill laced with fentanyl, believing it was a prescribed painkiller for a knee injury.
item.dc:creator: Brian Dorman, Fox 5

item.title: First rain and then fire chase people from their homes in North and South Carolina
item.description: When Nicole Taylor and her family moved to their new home in the South Carolina mountains six months ago, the gorgeous view of Table Rock Mountain was the clincher.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Organ transplant patient in Michigan dies from rabies
item.description: A Michigan resident died earlier this year after contracting rabies from an organ transplant, health officials said.
item.dc:creator: DEVNA BOSE AP Health Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: What we know about the deadly air crash between a passenger jet and a US Army helicopter
item.description: A congressional hearing Thursday reinforced the idea that the January midair collision between an American Airlines passenger jet and an Army helicopter that killed all 67 people aboard both aircraft could have been prevented.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Appeals court refuses to halt an order for the rehiring of thousands of fired federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An appeals court in California has refused to halt a judge’s order requiring the Trump administration to rehire thousands of federal workers who were let go in mass firings. A split 9th U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST and JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sundance Film Festival is leaving Utah for Colorado, starting in 2027
item.description: Boulder has officially been unveiled as the city to host the Sundance Film Festival starting in 2027, according to a press release from the Sundance Institute.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Kristensen, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Washington researchers warn of serious decline in honey bee colonies in 2025
item.description: Losses of commercial honey bee colonies are projected to be the highest on record in the last decade, according to Washington State University researchers.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Deml, Fox 5

item.title: Ex-North Dakota lawmaker gets 10 years for going to Europe with intent to pay for sex with a minor
item.description: A once-powerful former North Dakota lawmaker was sentenced Wednesday to 10 years in prison for traveling to Europe with the intent to pay for sex with a minor.
item.dc:creator: JACK DURA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chewing gum releases thousands of microplastics into body, study finds
item.description: A recent study found that chewing gum can release up to thousands of microplastics per piece and potentially be ingested, according to researchers.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dollar Tree sells Family Dollar for $1 billion
item.description: Dollar Tree has agreed to sell Family Dollar for $1 billion, ending the short-lived merger deal and allowing both companies to focus on their core operations.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: A year after the Baltimore bridge collapse, a long road to recovery is ahead
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — A year after the catastrophic collapse of Baltimore’s Francis Scott Key Bridge, Maryland leaders are honoring the six construction workers who were killed when the road they were repairing buckled underneath them.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Columbia student protester can't be detained for now as she fights deportation, judge rules
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A Columbia University student from South Korea who is facing potential deportation for her involvement in a pro-Palestinian protest can't be taken into immigration detention for now, a federal judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Do you eat your meal in less than 20 minutes? It might be time to slow down
item.description: Experts tend to focus on the kinds of foods you can eat to improve your health. But the speed at which you devour your dinner matters just as much.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge won't lift block on Trump use of Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans
item.description: U.S. District Court Judge James Boasberg declined to lift a restraining order barring the Trump administration from using the Alien Enemies Act to deport Venezuelans, determining migrants are likely to succeed in pushing for hearings on whether they are in a gang.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9
